[[東高野街道]]との交点。前後方向が竹内街道。（[[2007年]]1月撮影、写真の羽曳野市商工会館は現存せず）]] }} （西側から記述する） ; [[堺市]] * 堺（[[大小路]]交差点=[[大道筋]]交点） - 瓦町（[[堺市役所]]） - 榎元町（[[西高野街道]]と分岐） - [[大阪市交通局]]中百舌鳥検車場の北側 - [[ときはま線]]と交差 - [[金岡村 (大阪府) |金岡町]]（[[金岡神社]]） - [[大阪府道2号大阪中央環状線|大阪中央環状線]]と斜めに交差（この付近で[[難波大道]]と合流） - [[大泉緑地]]南沿い - 野遠町（[[下高野街道]]と交差） - [[西除川]]（西除橋） ; [[松原市]] * 岡町（丹南北交差点=[[国道309号]]交点） - 岡（丹南東交差点=[[中高野街道]]と交差） - 立部（立部西・立部東交差点=大阪中央環状線交点） ; [[羽曳野市]] * 野（野公民館前） - [[東除川]]（伊勢橋） - 天仁病院前 - [[ボケ山古墳]]（[[仁賢天皇]]陵）付近 - 軽里1丁目交点 - 白鳥交差点（[[国道170号]]交点） - [[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]前 - （[[国道166号]]） - [[上ノ太子駅]]前 ;[[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]] * 六枚橋交差点（太子町役場付近） - （旧街道） - [[太子町立竹内街道歴史資料館|竹内街道歴史資料館]] - 風鼻橋東詰交差点（[[道の駅近つ飛鳥の里太子]]付近） - [[竹内峠]] ; [[葛城市]] * 高峰神社付近 - （旧街道・竹内集落内） - 芭蕉旧跡綿弓塚 - 竹内交差点南 - [[長尾街道]]交点 - [[長尾神社]] - [[長尾]]交差点（以東は[[横大路 (奈良県)|横大路]]） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=浅井建爾 |edition= 初版|date=2001-11-10 |title=道と路がわかる辞典 |publisher=[[日本実業出版社]] |page=86|isbn=4-534-03315-X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book |和書 |author=ロム・インターナショナル（編） |date=2005-02-01 |title=道路地図 びっくり!博学知識 |publisher=[[河出書房新社]] |series=KAWADE夢文庫|pages=52-53|isbn=4-309-49566-4|ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古代道路]] * [[難波大道]] * [[長尾街道]] * [[太子町立竹内街道歴史資料館]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/doroseibi/kakusyusesaku/rekishikaidou.html#takenouchi 竹内街道ウォーキングマップ] - 大阪府のページ {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Road-stub}} {{History of transportation-stub}} {{国道166号}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけのうちかいとう}} [[Category:国道166号]] [[Category:街道]] [[Category:大阪府の交通史]] [[Category:奈良県の交通史]] [[Category:大阪府の道路]] [[Category:奈良県の道路]] [[Category:飛鳥時代]] [[Category:日本遺産]]

web|和書|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/event_info/nenkangyouji1/1534900110524.html|title=平成30年度年間行事|website=太子町ホームページ|publisher=[[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]|accessdate=2019-04-07}}。 == 概要 == 二上山の南麓を通って、[[大和国]]と[[河内国]]を結んだ古代の幹線道路の一つ{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001}}。日本最古の官道といわれる。大阪府堺市大小路から竹内峠を通り、奈良県葛城市に所在する長尾神社までの約26キロメートルの道が現存している（内大阪府下は23キロメートル）{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=52}}。両端の難波、飛鳥とも市街地になっていることから、かつて幅30 [[メートル]]あったとされる[[飛鳥時代]]の大道の面影は残されていない{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=53}}。街道沿いには、[[応神天皇#陵・霊廟|応神天皇陵]]、[[仁徳天皇#陵・霊廟|仁徳天皇陵]]、[[山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵]]をはじめとする[[古墳]]が多数あることから、物資輸送路、文化伝達路として重要な役割を果たした幹線通りと考えられている{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001}}。[[古市古墳群]]と[[百舌鳥古墳群]]のほぼ中央部を走る東西道路であり、2つの古墳群を繋ぐ道路であったとも考えられる。[[長尾街道]]より遅れて敷設されたと考えられる。また、この街道を直線道路として東西に延長すれば[[誉田山古墳]]南端の後円部に、[[大仙古墳]]の南東端部の前方部に接して通っていることになる。つまり、この二つの巨大古墳は地図上の[[北緯]]線上に造られていることが分かる。 竹内街道の名は、奈良県葛城市にある竹内集落を通って竹内峠を越えていくことに由来する{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001}}。 == 成り立ち・歴史 == 竹内街道は『[[日本書紀]]』の[[推古天皇]]21年（[[613年]]）の条に、後に[[上町台地]]に首都の[[難波宮]]が置かれる「難波（[[大阪市]]）より京（[[飛鳥]]）に至る大道（おおじ）を置く」と記されていた[[難波大道]]などと同様、日本最古の「'''官道'''」である{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001}}{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=52}}。一説には、[[聖徳太子]]が[[小野妹子]]らが中国大陸への使者として派遣された[[遣隋使]]が帰国の際に同行してくる大陸からの使者が通るために、立派な道路が必要だと考えて整備したものだといわれる{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=52}}。現在の竹内街道は、大部分は推古天皇時代の官道と重なっている。東側は[[奈良盆地]]南部を東西に横切る官道[[横大路 (奈良県)|横大路]]に繋がっている。[[天武天皇]]元年[[7月1日 (旧暦)|7月1日]]（[[672年]][[7月30日]]）の条に「会明に、西の方を臨み見れば、大津・丹比、両の道より、戦の衆多に至る」とみえ、[[壬申の乱]]にも使われていたことが分かり、長尾街道と竹内街道であると推定されている。 官道として整備されたのは上記の通り[[7世紀]]初め頃であるが、二上山の西麓（現在の大阪府[[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]）には[[4世紀]]から[[5世紀]]にかけての[[陵墓]]・[[古墳]]などの[[遺跡]]が数多く残っているため、既にかなりの人々の往来があったと思われる。 [[飛鳥時代]]には、[[遣隋使]]の使節や留学僧が往来し、難波から上陸した大陸の先進文化は、竹内街道を通って大和へ伝えられ[[飛鳥文化]]の発展をもたらしたほか、[[シルクロード]]から海路を通って伝来した仏教もまた、竹内街道を通って大和へ伝わり、日本文化のいしづえとなったといわれる{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001}}。 中世には[[伊勢街道]]の一部として存続し、[[1890年]]に府県道となり、[[1974年]]に[[国道166号]]に指定されている。したがって竹内街道は飛鳥時代より現在に至るまで街道として利用されていることになる。 近世には起点を堺[[大道筋]]（[[紀州街道]]）交点とした。[[江戸時代]]、沿道の竹内集落に[[松尾芭蕉]]が一時期住んでいた。現在、そこに芭蕉歌碑の綿弓塚があり、公園として整備されている。 == 経由地 （沿線施設等）== {{右| [[File:Ooshoji~daidousuji201512.JPG|thumb|225px|none|大小路交差点・堺市堺区
交差点から奥方向が竹内街道。（2015年12月撮影）]] [[画像:20041209竹之内街道02.JPG|thumb|225px|none|竹内街道・堺市北区黒土町
（[[2004年]]12月撮影）]] [[画像:羽曳野市古市四つ辻01.JPG|thumb|225px|none|蓑の辻（古市四つ辻・[[羽曳野市]]内）
[[東高野街道]]との交点。前後方向が竹内街道。（[[2007年]]1月撮影、写真の羽曳野市商工会館は現存せず）]] }} （西側から記述する） ; [[堺市]] * 堺（[[大小路]]交差点=[[大道筋]]交点） - 瓦町（[[堺市役所]]） - 榎元町（[[西高野街道]]と分岐） - [[大阪市交通局]]中百舌鳥検車場の北側 - [[ときはま線]]と交差 - [[金岡村 (大阪府) |金岡町]]（[[金岡神社]]） - [[大阪府道2号大阪中央環状線|大阪中央環状線]]と斜めに交差（この付近で[[難波大道]]と合流） - [[大泉緑地]]南沿い - 野遠町（[[下高野街道]]と交差） - [[西除川]]（西除橋） ; [[松原市]] * 岡町（丹南北交差点=[[国道309号]]交点） - 岡（丹南東交差点=[[中高野街道]]と交差） - 立部（立部西・立部東交差点=大阪中央環状線交点） ; [[羽曳野市]] * 野（野公民館前） - [[東除川]]（伊勢橋） - 天仁病院前 - [[ボケ山古墳]]（[[仁賢天皇]]陵）付近 - 軽里1丁目交点 - 白鳥交差点（[[国道170号]]交点） - [[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]前 - （[[国道166号]]） - [[上ノ太子駅]]前 ;[[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]] * 六枚橋交差点（太子町役場付近） - （旧街道） - [[太子町立竹内街道歴史資料館|竹内街道歴史資料館]] - 風鼻橋東詰交差点（[[道の駅近つ飛鳥の里太子]]付近） - [[竹内峠]] ; [[葛城市]] * 高峰神社付近 - （旧街道・竹内集落内） - 芭蕉旧跡綿弓塚 - 竹内交差点南 - [[長尾街道]]交点 - [[長尾神社]] - [[長尾]]交差点（以東は[[横大路 (奈良県)|横大路]]） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=浅井建爾 |edition= 初版|date=2001-11-10 |title=道と路がわかる辞典 |publisher=[[日本実業出版社]] |page=86|isbn=4-534-03315-X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book |和書 |author=ロム・インターナショナル（編） |date=2005-02-01 |title=道路地図 びっくり!博学知識 |publisher=[[河出書房新社]] |series=KAWADE夢文庫|pages=52-53|isbn=4-309-49566-4|ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古代道路]] * [[難波大道]] * [[長尾街道]] * [[太子町立竹内街道歴史資料館]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/doroseibi/kakusyusesaku/rekishikaidou.html#takenouchi 竹内街道ウォーキングマップ] - 大阪府のページ {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Road-stub}} {{History of transportation-stub}} {{国道166号}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけのうちかいとう}} [[Category:国道166号]] [[Category:街道]] [[Category:大阪府の交通史]] [[Category:奈良県の交通史]] [[Category:大阪府の道路]] [[Category:奈良県の道路]] [[Category:飛鳥時代]] [[Category:日本遺産]]

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2013年3月|ソートキー=道国166たけのうちかいとう}} {{右| [[画像:Takenouchi-Touge01.JPG|thumb|none|256px|[[竹内峠]]（大阪府・奈良県境）写真左は[[国道166号]]、右側が旧街道]] }} '''竹内街道'''（たけのうちかいどう）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]]から[[奈良県]][[葛城市]]の[[長尾神社]]付近に至る日本最古の[[日本の古代道路|古代官道]]である。かつては'''丹比道'''（たじひみち）と言われた{{Cite book|和書 |title=大阪の街道 |year=1989 |publisher=松籟社 |page=66}}。 [[羽曳野市]]の白鳥交差点から[[葛城市]]の竹内集落付近までの区間は、そのほとんどの区域が[[1974年]]から[[国道166号]]に指定されている。[[2017年]]には大阪で初めて[[日本遺産]]に認定{{Cite web|和書|title=祝 日本遺産認定！日本最古の国道「竹内街道・横大路（大道）」 {{!}} イベント情報 {{!}} 竹内街道・横大路|url=http://www.saikonokandou.com/2017/04/1470/|website=www.saikonokandou.com|accessdate=2019-04-04|language=ja}}。また、[[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]では一大イベントとして春に「太子聖燈会」、秋に「竹内街道灯路祭り」が毎年開催されている{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/event_info/nenkangyouji1/1534900110524.html|title=平成30年度年間行事|website=太子町ホームページ|publisher=[[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]|accessdate=2019-04-07}}。 == 概要 == [[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]の南麓を通って、[[大和国]]と[[河内国]]を結んだ古代の幹線道路の一つ{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001}}。日本最古の官道といわれる。大阪府堺市大小路から竹内峠を通り、奈良県葛城市に所在する長尾神社までの約26キロメートルの道が現存している（内大阪府下は23キロメートル）{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=52}}。両端の難波、飛鳥とも市街地になっていることから、かつて幅30 [[メートル]]あったとされる[[飛鳥時代]]の大道の面影は残されていない{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=53}}。街道沿いには、[[応神天皇#陵・霊廟|応神天皇陵]]、[[仁徳天皇#陵・霊廟|仁徳天皇陵]]、[[山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵]]をはじめとする[[古墳]]が多数あることから、物資輸送路、文化伝達路として重要な役割を果たした幹線通りと考えられている{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001}}。[[古市古墳群]]と[[百舌鳥古墳群]]のほぼ中央部を走る東西道路であり、2つの古墳群を繋ぐ道路であったとも考えられる。[[長尾街道]]より遅れて敷設されたと考えられる。また、この街道を直線道路として東西に延長すれば[[誉田山古墳]]南端の後円部に、[[大仙古墳]]の南東端部の前方部に接して通っていることになる。つまり、この二つの巨大古墳は地図上の[[北緯]]線上に造られていることが分かる。 竹内街道の名は、奈良県葛城市にある竹内集落を通って竹内峠を越えていくことに由来する{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001}}。 == 成り立ち・歴史 == (奈良県)|横大路]]に繋がっている。[[天武天皇]]元年[[7月1日 (旧暦)|7月1日]]（[[672年]][[7月30日]]）の条に「会明に、西の方を臨み見れば、大津・丹比、両の道より、戦の衆多に至る」とみえ、[[壬申の乱]]にも使われていたことが分かり、長尾街道と竹内街道であると推定されている。 官道として整備されたのは上記の通り[[7世紀]]初め頃であるが、二上山の西麓（現在の大阪府[[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]）には[[4世紀]]から[[5世紀]]にかけての[[陵墓]]・[[古墳]]などの[[遺跡]]が数多く残っているため、既にかなりの人々の往来があったと思われる。 [[飛鳥時代]]には、[[遣隋使]]の使節や留学僧が往来し、難波から上陸した大陸の先進文化は、竹内街道を通って大和へ伝えられ[[飛鳥文化]]の発展をもたらしたほか、[[シルクロード]]から海路を通って伝来した仏教もまた、竹内街道を通って大和へ伝わり、日本文化のいしづえとなったといわれる{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001}}。 近世には起点を堺[[大道筋]]（[[紀州街道]]）交点とした。[[江戸時代]]、沿道の竹内集落に[[松尾芭蕉]]が一時期住んでいた。現在、そこに芭蕉歌碑の綿弓塚があり、公園として整備されている。 == 経由地 （沿線施設等）== {{右| [[File:Ooshoji~daidousuji201512.JPG|thumb|225px|none|大小路交差点・堺市堺区
交差点から奥方向が竹内街道。（2015年12月撮影）]] [[画像:20041209竹之内街道02.JPG|thumb|225px|none|竹内街道・堺市北区黒土町
（[[2004年]]12月撮影）]] [[画像:羽曳野市古市四つ辻01.JPG|thumb|225px|none|蓑の辻（古市四つ辻・[[羽曳野市]]内）
[[東高野街道]]との交点。前後方向が竹内街道。（[[2007年]]1月撮影、写真の羽曳野市商工会館は現存せず）]] }} （西側から記述する） ; [[堺市]] * 堺（[[大小路]]交差点=[[大道筋]]交点） - 瓦町（[[堺市役所]]） - 榎元町（[[西高野街道]]と分岐） - [[大阪市交通局]]中百舌鳥検車場の北側 - [[ときはま線]]と交差 - [[金岡村 (大阪府) |金岡町]]（[[金岡神社]]） - [[大阪府道2号大阪中央環状線|大阪中央環状線]]と斜めに交差（この付近で[[難波大道]]と合流） - [[大泉緑地]]南沿い - 野遠町（[[下高野街道]]と交差） - [[西除川]]（西除橋） ; [[松原市]] * 岡町（丹南北交差点=[[国道309号]]交点） - 岡（丹南東交差点=[[中高野街道]]と交差） - 立部（立部西・立部東交差点=大阪中央環状線交点） ; [[羽曳野市]] * 野（野公民館前） - [[東除川]]（伊勢橋） - 天仁病院前 - [[ボケ山古墳]]（[[仁賢天皇]]陵）付近 - 軽里1丁目交点 - 白鳥交差点（[[国道170号]]交点） - [[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]前 - （[[国道166号]]） - [[上ノ太子駅]]前 ;[[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]] * 六枚橋交差点（太子町役場付近） - （旧街道） - [[太子町立竹内街道歴史資料館|竹内街道歴史資料館]] - 風鼻橋東詰交差点（[[道の駅近つ飛鳥の里太子]]付近） - [[竹内峠]] ; [[葛城市]] * 高峰神社付近 - （旧街道・竹内集落内） - 芭蕉旧跡綿弓塚 - 竹内交差点南 - [[長尾街道]]交点 - [[長尾神社]] - [[長尾]]交差点（以東は[[横大路 (奈良県)|横大路]]） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=浅井建爾 |edition= 初版|date=2001-11-10 |title=道と路がわかる辞典 |publisher=[[日本実業出版社]] |page=86|isbn=4-534-03315-X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book |和書 |author=ロム・インターナショナル（編） |date=2005-02-01 |title=道路地図 びっくり!博学知識 |publisher=[[河出書房新社]] |series=KAWADE夢文庫|pages=52-53|isbn=4-309-49566-4|ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古代道路]] * [[難波大道]] * [[長尾街道]] * [[太子町立竹内街道歴史資料館]] * [[小墾田宮]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/doroseibi/kakusyusesaku/rekishikaidou.html#takenouchi 竹内街道ウォーキングマップ] - 大阪府のページ {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Road-stub}} {{History of transportation-stub}} {{国道166号}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけのうちかいとう}} [[Category:国道166号]] [[Category:街道]] [[Category:大阪府の交通史]] [[Category:奈良県の交通史]] [[Category:大阪府の道路]] [[Category:奈良県の道路]] [[Category:飛鳥時代]] [[Category:日本遺産]]

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=March 2013|sortkey=道国166たけのうちかいとう}} {{right| [[File:Takenouchi-Touge01.JPG|thumb|none|256px|{{ill|Takenouchi Pass|ja|竹内峠|12=draft}} (at the Osaka-Nara prefectural border). The left side is part of {{ill|National Route 166|ja|国道166号|12=draft}}, while the right side shows the old highway.]] }} '''Takenouchi Highway''' (竹内街道) is the oldest ancient government road in Japan, stretching from {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|12=draft}} in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} to the area near {{ill|Nagao Shrine|ja|長尾神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Katsuragi|ja|葛城市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. Historically, it was known as '''Tajihi Road''' (丹比道).<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Osaka no Kaidō (大阪の街道)|year=1989|publisher=Shōraisha|page=66}}</ref>
From the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=draft}} in {{ill|Habikino|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}} to the vicinity of the Takenouchi settlement in Katsuragi, much of the route was designated as {{ill|National Route 166|ja|国道166号|12=draft}} in 1974. In 2017, it became the first {{ill|Japan Heritage|ja|日本遺産|12=draft}} site in Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|title=Japan Heritage Recognition! Japan's Oldest National Route "Takenouchi Highway and Yokooji"|url=http://www.saikonokandou.com/2017/04/1470/|website=Saikonokandou.com|accessdate=2019-04-04}}</ref> Additionally, in {{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}, major events such as the "Taishi Sacred Lantern Gathering" in spring and the "Takenouchi Highway Lantern Festival" in autumn are held annually.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/event_info/nenkangyouji1/1534900110524.html|title=FY2018 Annual Events|website=Taishi Town Official Website|publisher={{ill|Taishi|ja|太子町 (大阪府)|12=draft}}|accessdate=2019-04-07}}</ref>
== Overview ==
The Takenouchi Highway is an ancient arterial road that traverses the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|12=draft}}, connecting {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} with {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}}. It is regarded as Japan's oldest {{ill|ancient highway|ja|古代官道|12=draft}}. Extending approximately 26 kilometers from the Daishōji area of Sakai to Nagao Shrine in Katsuragi (23 kilometers within Osaka Prefecture), it retains remnants of its original path.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=Road Map: Amazing Knowledge Encyclopedia (道路location map びっくり!博学知識)|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha|series=Kawade Yume Bunko|pages=52–53|isbn=4-309-49566-4}}</ref> Though the bustling cityscapes of Naniwa and Asuka have obscured its original width (once up to 30 meters during the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}), the highway's historical significance as a transportation and cultural transmission route remains evident through its proximity to numerous ancient Kofuns, such as.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=Dictionary of Roads and Paths (道と路がわかる辞典)|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha|isbn=4-534-03315-X}}</ref>
* '''{{ill|Kondayama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|lt=Emperor Ōjin's Mausoleum|ja|応神天皇陵|12=draft}}''' * '''{{ill|Daisen Kofun|fi|Daisenry%C5%8D-kofun|pt|Daisenry%C5%8D-Kofun|fr|Kofun Daisenry%C5%8D|es|Daisenry%C5%8D-Kofun|en|Daisen Kofun|ca|Kofun Daisenry%C5%8D|ko|%EB%8B%A4%EC%9D%B4%EC%84%BC%EB%A6%AD %EA%B3%A0%EB%B6%84|zh|%E5%A4%A7%E4%BB%99%E9%99%B5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|lt=Emperor Nintoku's Mausoleum|ja|仁徳天皇陵|12=draft}}''' * '''{{ill|Empress Suiko's Mausoleum|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|lt=Empress Suiko's Mausoleum (Yamada Takatsuka Kofun)|12=draft}}'''
The highway's name derives from the Takenouchi settlement near the {{ill|Takenouchi Pass|ja|竹内峠|12=draft}}, located in Katsuragi.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}
== History ==
The Takenouchi Highway is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} under the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}} in 613 CE. It describes the establishment of a "grand road" connecting Naniwa (modern {{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪市|12=draft}}) to the capital in Asuka.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=道と路がわかる辞典|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha}}</ref> One theory suggests that {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft}} commissioned the highway to facilitate diplomatic exchanges with the mainland, especially for envoys such as {{ill|Ono no Imoko|ja|小野妹子|12=draft}} during missions to the {{ill|Sui dynasty|ja|隋|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=道路location map びっくり!博学知識|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha}}</ref>
During the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}, the highway served as a conduit for the cultural and technological advancements brought by envoys and monks from the mainland. It played a pivotal role in transmitting the advanced culture and Buddhism that became foundational to Japanese civilization.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}
In the {{ill|Middle Ages|ja|中世|12=draft}}, it remained a vital route as part of the {{ill|Ise Road|ja|伊勢街道|12=draft}}. By 1890, it had been designated a prefectural road, and in 1974, it became part of {{ill|National Route 166|ja|国道166号|12=draft}}.{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=52}} Thus, the highway has been in continuous use from the Asuka period to the present.
== Major Landmarks == {{right| [[File:Ooshoji~daidousuji201512.JPG|thumb|225px|none|Daishōji Intersection, Sakai City (2015)]] [[File:20041209竹之内街道02.JPG|thumb|225px|none|Takenouchi Highway, Sakai City]] }} ; {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|12=draft}} * Daishōji Intersection (starting point) * Kōno Shrine * {{ill|Gold Spring Shrine|ja|金岡神社|12=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|title=道と路がわかる辞典|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha|date=2001}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|title=道路location map びっくり!博学知識|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha|date=2005}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ancient Japanese roads|ja|日本の古代道路|12=draft}} * {{ill|Naniwa Grand Avenue|ja|難波大道|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nagao Highway|ja|長尾街道|12=draft}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2013年3月|ソートキー=道国166たけのうちかいとう}} {{右| [[画像:Takenouchi-Touge01.JPG|thumb|none|256px|[[竹内峠]]（大阪府・奈良県境）写真左は[[国道166号]]、右側が旧街道]] }} '''竹内街道'''（たけのうちかいどう）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]]から[[奈良県]][[葛城市]]の[[長尾神社]]付近に至る日本最古の[[日本の古代道路|古代官道]]である。かつては'''丹比道'''（たじひみち）と言われた{{Cite book|和書 |title=大阪の街道 |year=1989 |publisher=松籟社 |page=66}}。 [[羽曳野市]]の白鳥交差点から[[葛城市]]の竹内集落付近までの区間は、そのほとんどの区域が[[1974年]]から[[国道166号]]に指定されている。[[2017年]]には大阪で初めて[[日本遺産]]に認定{{Cite web|和書|title=祝 日本遺産認定！日本最古の国道「竹内街道・横大路（大道）」 {{!}} イベント情報 {{!}} 竹内街道・横大路|url=http://www.saikonokandou.com/2017/04/1470/|website=www.saikonokandou.com|accessdate=2019-04-04|language=ja}}。また、[[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]では一大イベントとして春に「太子聖燈会」、秋に「竹内街道灯路祭り」が毎年開催されている{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/event_info/nenkangyouji1/1534900110524.html|title=平成30年度年間行事|website=太子町ホームページ|publisher=[[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]|accessdate=2019-04-07}}。 == 概要 == [[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]の南麓を通って、[[大和国]]と[[河内国]]を結んだ古代の幹線道路の一つ{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001}}。日本最古の官道といわれる。大阪府堺市大小路から竹内峠を通り、奈良県葛城市に所在する長尾神社までの約26キロメートルの道が現存している（内大阪府下は23キロメートル）{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=52}}。両端の難波、飛鳥とも市街地になっていることから、かつて幅30 [[メートル]]あったとされる[[飛鳥時代]]の大道の面影は残されていない{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=53}}。街道沿いには、[[応神天皇#陵・霊廟|応神天皇陵]]、[[仁徳天皇#陵・霊廟|仁徳天皇陵]]、[[山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵]]をはじめとする[[古墳]]が多数あることから、物資輸送路、文化伝達路として重要な役割を果たした幹線通りと考えられている{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001}}。[[古市古墳群]]と[[百舌鳥古墳群]]のほぼ中央部を走る東西道路であり、2つの古墳群を繋ぐ道路であったとも考えられる。[[長尾街道]]より遅れて敷設されたと考えられる。また、この街道を直線道路として東西に延長すれば[[誉田山古墳]]南端の後円部に、[[大仙古墳]]の南東端部の前方部に接して通っていることになる。つまり、この二つの巨大古墳は地図上の[[北緯]]線上に造られていることが分かる。 竹内街道の名は、奈良県葛城市にある竹内集落を通って竹内峠を越えていくことに由来する{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001}}。 == 成り立ち・歴史 == 竹内街道は『[[日本書紀]]』の[[推古天皇]]21年（[[613年]]）の条に、後に[[上町台地]]に首都の[[難波宮]]が置かれる「難波（[[大阪市]]）より京（[[飛鳥]]）に至る大道（おおじ）を置く」と記されていた[[難波大道]]などと同様、日本最古の「'''官道'''」である{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001}}{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=52}}。一説には、[[聖徳太子]]が[[小野妹子]]らが中国大陸への使者として派遣された[[遣隋使]]が帰国の際に同行してくる大陸からの使者が通るために、立派な道路が必要だと考えて整備したものだといわれる{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=52}}。現在の竹内街道は、大部分は推古天皇時代の官道と重なっている。東側は[[奈良盆地]]南部を東西に横切る官道[[横大路 (奈良県)|横大路]]に繋がっている。[[天武天皇]]元年[[7月1日 (旧暦)|7月1日]]（[[672年]][[7月30日]]）の条に「会明に、西の方を臨み見れば、大津・丹比、両の道より、戦の衆多に至る」とみえ、[[壬申の乱]]にも使われていたことが分かり、長尾街道と竹内街道であると推定されている。 官道として整備されたのは上記の通り[[7世紀]]初め頃であるが、二上山の西麓（現在の大阪府[[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]）には[[4世紀]]から[[5世紀]]にかけての[[陵墓]]・[[古墳]]などの[[遺跡]]が数多く残っているため、既にかなりの人々の往来があったと思われる。 [[飛鳥時代]]には、[[遣隋使]]の使節や留学僧が往来し、難波から上陸した大陸の先進文化は、竹内街道を通って大和へ伝えられ[[飛鳥文化]]の発展をもたらしたほか、[[シルクロード]]から海路を通って伝来した仏教もまた、竹内街道を通って大和へ伝わり、日本文化のいしづえとなったといわれる{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001}}。 中世には[[伊勢街道]]の一部として存続し、[[1890年]]に府県道となり、[[1974年]]に[[国道166号]]に指定されている。したがって竹内街道は飛鳥時代より現在に至るまで街道として利用されていることになる。 近世には起点を堺[[大道筋]]（[[紀州街道]]）交点とした。[[江戸時代]]、沿道の竹内集落に[[松尾芭蕉]]が一時期住んでいた。現在、そこに芭蕉歌碑の綿弓塚があり、公園として整備されている。 == 経由地 （沿線施設等）== {{右| [[File:Ooshoji~daidousuji201512.JPG|thumb|225px|none|大小路交差点・堺市堺区
交差点から奥方向が竹内街道。（2015年12月撮影）]] [[画像:20041209竹之内街道02.JPG|thumb|225px|none|竹内街道・堺市北区黒土町
（[[2004年]]12月撮影）]] [[画像:羽曳野市古市四つ辻01.JPG|thumb|225px|none|蓑の辻（古市四つ辻・[[羽曳野市]]内）
[[東高野街道]]との交点。前後方向が竹内街道。（[[2007年]]1月撮影、写真の羽曳野市商工会館は現存せず）]] }} （西側から記述する） ; [[堺市]] * 堺（[[大小路]]交差点=[[大道筋]]交点） - 瓦町（[[堺市役所]]） - 榎元町（[[西高野街道]]と分岐） - [[大阪市交通局]]中百舌鳥検車場の北側 - [[ときはま線]]と交差 - [[金岡村 (大阪府) |金岡町]]（[[金岡神社]]） - [[大阪府道2号大阪中央環状線|大阪中央環状線]]と斜めに交差（この付近で[[難波大道]]と合流） - [[大泉緑地]]南沿い - 野遠町（[[下高野街道]]と交差） - [[西除川]]（西除橋） ; [[松原市]] * 岡町（丹南北交差点=[[国道309号]]交点） - 岡（丹南東交差点=[[中高野街道]]と交差） - 立部（立部西・立部東交差点=大阪中央環状線交点） ; [[羽曳野市]] * 野（野公民館前） - [[東除川]]（伊勢橋） - 天仁病院前 - [[ボケ山古墳]]（[[仁賢天皇]]陵）付近 - 軽里1丁目交点 - 白鳥交差点（[[国道170号]]交点） - [[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]前 - （[[国道166号]]） - [[上ノ太子駅]]前 ;[[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]] * 六枚橋交差点（太子町役場付近） - （旧街道） - [[太子町立竹内街道歴史資料館|竹内街道歴史資料館]] - 風鼻橋東詰交差点（[[道の駅近つ飛鳥の里太子]]付近） - [[竹内峠]] ; [[葛城市]] * 高峰神社付近 - （旧街道・竹内集落内） - 芭蕉旧跡綿弓塚 - 竹内交差点南 - [[長尾街道]]交点 - [[長尾神社]] - [[長尾]]交差点（以東は[[横大路 (奈良県)|横大路]]） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=浅井建爾 |edition= 初版|date=2001-11-10 |title=道と路がわかる辞典 |publisher=[[日本実業出版社]] |page=86|isbn=4-534-03315-X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book |和書 |author=ロム・インターナショナル（編） |date=2005-02-01 |title=道路地図 びっくり!博学知識 |publisher=[[河出書房新社]] |series=KAWADE夢文庫|pages=52-53|isbn=4-309-49566-4|ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古代道路]] * [[難波大道]] * [[長尾街道]] * [[太子町立竹内街道歴史資料館]] * [[小墾田宮]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/doroseibi/kakusyusesaku/rekishikaidou.html#takenouchi 竹内街道ウォーキングマップ] - 大阪府のページ {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Road-stub}} {{History of transportation-stub}} {{国道166号}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけのうちかいとう}} [[Category:国道166号]] [[Category:街道]] [[Category:大阪府の交通史]] [[Category:奈良県の交通史]] [[Category:大阪府の道路]] [[Category:奈良県の道路]] [[Category:飛鳥時代]] [[Category:日本遺産]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[竹内街道]] to [[Takenouchi Kaidō]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2013年3月|ソートキー=道国166たけのうちかいとう}} {{右| [[画像:Takenouchi-Touge01.JPG|thumb|none|256px|[[竹内峠]]（大阪府・奈良県境）写真左は[[国道166号]]、右側が旧街道]] }} '''竹内街道'''（たけのうちかいどう）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]]から[[奈良県]][[葛城市]]の[[長尾神社]]付近に至る日本最古の[[日本の古代道路|古代官道]]である。かつては'''丹比道'''（たじひみち）と言われた{{Cite book|和書 |title=大阪の街道 |year=1989 |publisher=松籟社 |page=66}}。 [[羽曳野市]]の白鳥交差点から[[葛城市]]の竹内集落付近までの区間は、そのほとんどの区域が[[1974年]]から[[国道166号]]に指定されている。[[2017年]]には大阪で初めて[[日本遺産]]に認定{{Cite web|和書|title=祝 日本遺産認定！日本最古の国道「竹内街道・横大路（大道）」 {{!}} イベント情報 {{!}} 竹内街道・横大路|url=http://www.saikonokandou.com/2017/04/1470/|website=www.saikonokandou.com|accessdate=2019-04-04|language=ja}}。また、[[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]では一大イベントとして春に「太子聖燈会」、秋に「竹内街道灯路祭り」が毎年開催されている{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/event_info/nenkangyouji1/1534900110524.html|title=平成30年度年間行事|website=太子町ホームページ|publisher=[[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]|accessdate=2019-04-07}}。 == 概要 == [[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]の南麓を通って、[[大和国]]と[[河内国]]を結んだ古代の幹線道路の一つ{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001}}。日本最古の官道といわれる。大阪府堺市大小路から竹内峠を通り、奈良県葛城市に所在する長尾神社までの約26キロメートルの道が現存している（内大阪府下は23キロメートル）{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=52}}。両端の難波、飛鳥とも市街地になっていることから、かつて幅30 [[メートル]]あったとされる[[飛鳥時代]]の大道の面影は残されていない{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=53}}。街道沿いには、[[応神天皇#陵・霊廟|応神天皇陵]]、[[仁徳天皇#陵・霊廟|仁徳天皇陵]]、[[山田高塚古墳|推古天皇陵]]をはじめとする[[古墳]]が多数あることから、物資輸送路、文化伝達路として重要な役割を果たした幹線通りと考えられている{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001}}。[[古市古墳群]]と[[百舌鳥古墳群]]のほぼ中央部を走る東西道路であり、2つの古墳群を繋ぐ道路であったとも考えられる。[[長尾街道]]より遅れて敷設されたと考えられる。また、この街道を直線道路として東西に延長すれば[[誉田山古墳]]南端の後円部に、[[大仙古墳]]の南東端部の前方部に接して通っていることになる。つまり、この二つの巨大古墳は地図上の[[北緯]]線上に造られていることが分かる。 竹内街道の名は、奈良県葛城市にある竹内集落を通って竹内峠を越えていくことに由来する{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001}}。 == 成り立ち・歴史 == 竹内街道は『[[日本書紀]]』の[[推古天皇]]21年（[[613年]]）の条に、後に[[上町台地]]に首都の[[難波宮]]が置かれる「難波（[[大阪市]]）より京（[[飛鳥]]）に至る大道（おおじ）を置く」と記されていた[[難波大道]]などと同様、日本最古の「'''官道'''」である{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001}}{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=52}}。一説には、[[聖徳太子]]が[[小野妹子]]らが中国大陸への使者として派遣された[[遣隋使]]が帰国の際に同行してくる大陸からの使者が通るために、立派な道路が必要だと考えて整備したものだといわれる{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=52}}。現在の竹内街道は、大部分は推古天皇時代の官道と重なっている。東側は[[奈良盆地]]南部を東西に横切る官道[[横大路 (奈良県)|横大路]]に繋がっている。[[天武天皇]]元年[[7月1日 (旧暦)|7月1日]]（[[672年]][[7月30日]]）の条に「会明に、西の方を臨み見れば、大津・丹比、両の道より、戦の衆多に至る」とみえ、[[壬申の乱]]にも使われていたことが分かり、長尾街道と竹内街道であると推定されている。 官道として整備されたのは上記の通り[[7世紀]]初め頃であるが、二上山の西麓（現在の大阪府[[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]）には[[4世紀]]から[[5世紀]]にかけての[[陵墓]]・[[古墳]]などの[[遺跡]]が数多く残っているため、既にかなりの人々の往来があったと思われる。 [[飛鳥時代]]には、[[遣隋使]]の使節や留学僧が往来し、難波から上陸した大陸の先進文化は、竹内街道を通って大和へ伝えられ[[飛鳥文化]]の発展をもたらしたほか、[[シルクロード]]から海路を通って伝来した仏教もまた、竹内街道を通って大和へ伝わり、日本文化のいしづえとなったといわれる{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001}}。 中世には[[伊勢街道]]の一部として存続し、[[1890年]]に府県道となり、[[1974年]]に[[国道166号]]に指定されている。したがって竹内街道は飛鳥時代より現在に至るまで街道として利用されていることになる。 近世には起点を堺[[大道筋]]（[[紀州街道]]）交点とした。[[江戸時代]]、沿道の竹内集落に[[松尾芭蕉]]が一時期住んでいた。現在、そこに芭蕉歌碑の綿弓塚があり、公園として整備されている。 == 経由地 （沿線施設等）== {{右| [[File:Ooshoji~daidousuji201512.JPG|thumb|225px|none|大小路交差点・堺市堺区
交差点から奥方向が竹内街道。（2015年12月撮影）]] [[画像:20041209竹之内街道02.JPG|thumb|225px|none|竹内街道・堺市北区黒土町
（[[2004年]]12月撮影）]] [[画像:羽曳野市古市四つ辻01.JPG|thumb|225px|none|蓑の辻（古市四つ辻・[[羽曳野市]]内）
[[東高野街道]]との交点。前後方向が竹内街道。（[[2007年]]1月撮影、写真の羽曳野市商工会館は現存せず）]] }} （西側から記述する） ; [[堺市]] * 堺（[[大小路]]交差点=[[大道筋]]交点） - 瓦町（[[堺市役所]]） - 榎元町（[[西高野街道]]と分岐） - [[大阪市交通局]]中百舌鳥検車場の北側 - [[ときはま線]]と交差 - [[金岡村 (大阪府) |金岡町]]（[[金岡神社]]） - [[大阪府道2号大阪中央環状線|大阪中央環状線]]と斜めに交差（この付近で[[難波大道]]と合流） - [[大泉緑地]]南沿い - 野遠町（[[下高野街道]]と交差） - [[西除川]]（西除橋） ; [[松原市]] * 岡町（丹南北交差点=[[国道309号]]交点） - 岡（丹南東交差点=[[中高野街道]]と交差） - 立部（立部西・立部東交差点=大阪中央環状線交点） ; [[羽曳野市]] * 野（野公民館前） - [[東除川]]（伊勢橋） - 天仁病院前 - [[ボケ山古墳]]（[[仁賢天皇]]陵）付近 - 軽里1丁目交点 - 白鳥交差点（[[国道170号]]交点） - [[古市駅 (大阪府)|古市駅]]前 - （[[国道166号]]） - [[上ノ太子駅]]前 ;[[南河内郡]][[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]] * 六枚橋交差点（太子町役場付近） - （旧街道） - [[太子町立竹内街道歴史資料館|竹内街道歴史資料館]] - 風鼻橋東詰交差点（[[道の駅近つ飛鳥の里太子]]付近） - [[竹内峠]] ; [[葛城市]] * 高峰神社付近 - （旧街道・竹内集落内） - 芭蕉旧跡綿弓塚 - 竹内交差点南 - [[長尾街道]]交点 - [[長尾神社]] - [[長尾]]交差点（以東は[[横大路 (奈良県)|横大路]]） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=浅井建爾 |edition= 初版|date=2001-11-10 |title=道と路がわかる辞典 |publisher=[[日本実業出版社]] |page=86|isbn=4-534-03315-X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book |和書 |author=ロム・インターナショナル（編） |date=2005-02-01 |title=道路地図 びっくり!博学知識 |publisher=[[河出書房新社]] |series=KAWADE夢文庫|pages=52-53|isbn=4-309-49566-4|ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古代道路]] * [[難波大道]] * [[長尾街道]] * [[太子町立竹内街道歴史資料館]] * [[小墾田宮]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/doroseibi/kakusyusesaku/rekishikaidou.html#takenouchi 竹内街道ウォーキングマップ] - 大阪府のページ {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Road-stub}} {{History of transportation-stub}} {{国道166号}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけのうちかいとう}} [[Category:国道166号]] [[Category:街道]] [[Category:大阪府の交通史]] [[Category:奈良県の交通史]] [[Category:大阪府の道路]] [[Category:奈良県の道路]] [[Category:飛鳥時代]] [[Category:日本遺産]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:竹内街道]][[zh:竹內街道]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11598968]]
Wikidata name: Takenouchi Kaidō
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Citation needed|date=March 2013|sortkey=道国166たけのうちかいとう}}
{{right| 
[[File:Takenouchi-Touge01.JPG|thumb|none|256px|{{ill|Takenouchi Pass|ja|竹内峠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Pass|qq=|1=Takenouchi Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598871}} (at the Osaka-Nara prefectural border). The left side is part of [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]], while the right side shows the old highway.]]
}}
'''Takenouchi Highway''' (竹内街道) is the oldest ancient government road in Japan, stretching from [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] to the area near [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] in [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. Historically, it was known as '''Tajihi Road''' (丹比道).<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Osaka no Kaidō (大阪の街道)|year=1989|publisher=Shōraisha|page=66}}</ref>

From the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]] to the vicinity of the Takenouchi settlement in Katsuragi, much of the route was designated as [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]] in 1974. In 2017, it became the first [[:en:Japan Heritage|Japan Heritage]] site in Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|title=Japan Heritage Recognition! Japan's Oldest National Route "Takenouchi Highway and Yokooji"|url=http://www.saikonokandou.com/2017/04/1470/|website=Saikonokandou.com|accessdate=2019-04-04}}</ref> Additionally, in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi]], major events such as the "Taishi Sacred Lantern Gathering" in spring and the "Takenouchi Highway Lantern Festival" in autumn are held annually.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/event_info/nenkangyouji1/1534900110524.html|title=FY2018 Annual Events|website=Taishi Town Official Website|publisher=[[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi]]|accessdate=2019-04-07}}</ref>

== Overview ==

The Takenouchi Highway is an ancient arterial road that traverses the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, connecting [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] with [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]. It is regarded as Japan's oldest {{ill|ancient highway|ja|古代官道|1=kandō|lt=Ancient Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient highway|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaidō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官道|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452141}}. Extending approximately 26 kilometers from the Daishōji area of Sakai to Nagao Shrine in Katsuragi (23 kilometers within Osaka Prefecture), it retains remnants of its original path.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=Road Map: Amazing Knowledge Encyclopedia (道路location map びっくり!博学知識)|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha|series=Kawade Yume Bunko|pages=52–53|isbn=4-309-49566-4}}</ref> Though the bustling cityscapes of Naniwa and Asuka have obscured its original width (once up to 30 meters during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]), the highway's historical significance as a transportation and cultural transmission route remains evident through its proximity to numerous ancient Kofuns, such as.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=Dictionary of Roads and Paths (道と路がわかる辞典)|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha|isbn=4-534-03315-X}}</ref>

* '''{{ill|Kondayama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|lt=Emperor Ōjin's Mausoleum|ja|応神天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}'''
* '''{{ill|Daisen Kofun|fi|Daisenry%C5%8D-kofun|pt|Daisenry%C5%8D-Kofun|fr|Kofun Daisenry%C5%8D|es|Daisenry%C5%8D-Kofun|en|Daisen Kofun|ca|Kofun Daisenry%C5%8D|ko|%EB%8B%A4%EC%9D%B4%EC%84%BC%EB%A6%AD %EA%B3%A0%EB%B6%84|zh|%E5%A4%A7%E4%BB%99%E9%99%B5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|lt=Emperor Nintoku's Mausoleum|ja|仁徳天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''
* '''{{ill|Empress Suiko's Mausoleum|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|lt=Empress Suiko's Mausoleum (Yamada Takatsuka Kofun)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko's Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}'''

The highway's name derives from the Takenouchi settlement near the {{ill|Takenouchi Pass|ja|竹内峠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Pass|qq=|1=Takenouchi Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598871}}, located in Katsuragi.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

== History ==

The Takenouchi Highway is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] under the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] in 613 CE. It describes the establishment of a "grand road" connecting Naniwa (modern [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]]) to the capital in Asuka.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=道と路がわかる辞典|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha}}</ref> One theory suggests that [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] commissioned the highway to facilitate diplomatic exchanges with the mainland, especially for envoys such as [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] during missions to the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]].<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=道路location map びっくり!博学知識|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha}}</ref>

During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the highway served as a conduit for the cultural and technological advancements brought by envoys and monks from the mainland. It played a pivotal role in transmitting the advanced culture and Buddhism that became foundational to Japanese civilization.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

In the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages]], it remained a vital route as part of the {{ill|Ise Road|ja|伊勢街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Road|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢参宮街道|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}. By 1890, it had been designated a prefectural road, and in 1974, it became part of [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]].{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=52}} Thus, the highway has been in continuous use from the Asuka period to the present.

'''Takenouchi Highway''' (竹内街道) is the oldest ancient government road in Japan, stretching from [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] to the area near [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] in [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. Historically, it was known as '''Tajihi Road''' (丹比道).<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Osaka no Kaidō (大阪の街道)|year=1989|publisher=Shōraisha|page=66}}</ref>

From the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]] to the vicinity of the Takenouchi settlement in Katsuragi, much of the route was designated as [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]] in 1974. In 2017, it became the first [[:en:Japan Heritage|Japan Heritage]] site in Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|title=Japan Heritage Recognition! Japan's Oldest National Route "Takenouchi Highway and Yokooji"|url=http://www.saikonokandou.com/2017/04/1470/|website=Saikonokandou.com|accessdate=2019-04-04}}</ref> Additionally, in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi]], major events such as the "Taishi Sacred Lantern Gathering" in spring and the "Takenouchi Highway Lantern Festival" in autumn are held annually.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/event_info/nenkangyouji1/1534900110524.html|title=FY2018 Annual Events|website=Taishi Town Official Website|publisher=[[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi]]|accessdate=2019-04-07}}</ref>

== Overview ==

The Takenouchi Highway is an ancient arterial road that traverses the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, connecting [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] with [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]. It is regarded as Japan's oldest {{ill|ancient highway|ja|古代官道|1=kandō|lt=Ancient Kaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient highway|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaidō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官道|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452141}}. Extending approximately 26 kilometers from the Daishōji area of Sakai to Nagao Shrine in Katsuragi (23 kilometers within Osaka Prefecture), it retains remnants of its original path.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=Road Map: Amazing Knowledge Encyclopedia (道路location map びっくり!博学知識)|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha|series=Kawade Yume Bunko|pages=52–53|isbn=4-309-49566-4}}</ref> Though the bustling cityscapes of Naniwa and Asuka have obscured its original width (once up to 30 meters during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]), the highway's historical significance as a transportation and cultural transmission route remains evident through its proximity to numerous ancient Kofuns, such as.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=Dictionary of Roads and Paths (道と路がわかる辞典)|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha|isbn=4-534-03315-X}}</ref>

* '''{{ill|Kondayama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|lt=Emperor Ōjin's Mausoleum|ja|応神天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}'''
* '''{{ill|Daisen Kofun|fi|Daisenry%C5%8D-kofun|pt|Daisenry%C5%8D-Kofun|fr|Kofun Daisenry%C5%8D|es|Daisenry%C5%8D-Kofun|en|Daisen Kofun|ca|Kofun Daisenry%C5%8D|ko|%EB%8B%A4%EC%9D%B4%EC%84%BC%EB%A6%AD %EA%B3%A0%EB%B6%84|zh|%E5%A4%A7%E4%BB%99%E9%99%B5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|lt=Emperor Nintoku's Mausoleum|ja|仁徳天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''
* '''{{ill|Empress Suiko's Mausoleum|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|lt=Empress Suiko's Mausoleum (Yamada Takatsuka Kofun)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko's Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}'''

The highway's name derives from the Takenouchi settlement near the {{ill|Takenouchi Pass|ja|竹内峠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Pass|qq=|1=Takenouchi Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598871}}, located in Katsuragi.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

== History ==

The Takenouchi Highway is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] under the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] in 613 CE. It describes the establishment of a "grand road" connecting Naniwa (modern [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]]) to the capital in Asuka.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=道と路がわかる辞典|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha}}</ref> One theory suggests that [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] commissioned the highway to facilitate diplomatic exchanges with the mainland, especially for envoys such as [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] during missions to the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]].<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=道路location map びっくり!博学知識|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha}}</ref>

During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the highway served as a conduit for the cultural and technological advancements brought by envoys and monks from the mainland. It played a pivotal role in transmitting the advanced culture and Buddhism that became foundational to Japanese civilization.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

In the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages]], it remained a vital route as part of the {{ill|Ise Road|ja|伊勢街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Road|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢参宮街道|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}. '''Takenouchi Highway''' (竹内街道) is the oldest ancient government road in Japan, stretching from [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] to the area near [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] in [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. Historically, it was known as '''Tajihi Road''' (丹比道).<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Osaka no Kaidō (大阪の街道)|year=1989|publisher=Shōraisha|page=66}}</ref>

From the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]] to the vicinity of the Takenouchi settlement in Katsuragi, much of the route was designated as [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]] in 1974. In 2017, it became the first [[:en:Japan Heritage|Japan Heritage]] site in Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|title=Japan Heritage Recognition! Japan's Oldest National Route "Takenouchi Highway and Yokooji"|url=http://www.saikonokandou.com/2017/04/1470/|website=Saikonokandou.com|accessdate=2019-04-04}}</ref> Additionally, in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi]], major events such as the "Taishi Sacred Lantern Gathering" in spring and the "Takenouchi Highway Lantern Festival" in autumn are held annually.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/event_info/nenkangyouji1/1534900110524.html|title=FY2018 Annual Events|website=Taishi Town Official Website|publisher=[[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi]]|accessdate=2019-04-07}}</ref>

== Overview ==

The Takenouchi Highway is an ancient arterial road that traverses the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, connecting [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] with [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]. It is regarded as Japan's oldest {{ill|ancient highway|ja|古代官道|1=kandō|lt=Ancient Kaidō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient highway|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaidō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官道|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452141}}. Extending approximately 26 kilometers from the Daishōji area of Sakai to Nagao Shrine in Katsuragi (23 kilometers within Osaka Prefecture), it retains remnants of its original path.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=Road Map: Amazing Knowledge Encyclopedia (道路location map びっくり!博学知識)|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha|series=Kawade Yume Bunko|pages=52–53|isbn=4-309-49566-4}}</ref> Though the bustling cityscapes of Naniwa and Asuka have obscured its original width (once up to 30 meters during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]), the highway's historical significance as a transportation and cultural transmission route remains evident through its proximity to numerous ancient Kofuns, such as.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=Dictionary of Roads and Paths (道と路がわかる辞典)|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha|isbn=4-534-03315-X}}</ref>

* '''{{ill|Kondayama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|lt=Emperor Ōjin's Mausoleum|ja|応神天皇陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}'''
* '''{{ill|Daisen Kofun|fi|Daisenry%C5%8D-kofun|pt|Daisenry%C5%8D-Kofun|fr|Kofun Daisenry%C5%8D|es|Daisenry%C5%8D-Kofun|en|Daisen Kofun|ca|Kofun Daisenry%C5%8D|ko|%EB%8B%A4%EC%9D%B4%EC%84%BC%EB%A6%AD %EA%B3%A0%EB%B6%84|zh|%E5%A4%A7%E4%BB%99%E9%99%B5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|lt=Emperor Nintoku's Mausoleum|ja|仁徳天皇陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''
* '''{{ill|Empress Suiko's Mausoleum|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|lt=Empress Suiko's Mausoleum (Yamada Takatsuka Kofun)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko's Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}'''

The highway's name derives from the Takenouchi settlement near the {{ill|Takenouchi Pass|ja|竹内峠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Pass|1=Takenouchi Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598871}}, located in Katsuragi.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

== History ==

The Takenouchi Highway is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] under the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] in 613 CE. It describes the establishment of a "grand road" connecting Naniwa (modern [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]]) to the capital in Asuka.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=道と路がわかる辞典|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha}}</ref> One theory suggests that [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] commissioned the highway to facilitate diplomatic exchanges with the mainland, especially for envoys such as [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] during missions to the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]].<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=道路location map びっくり!博学知識|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha}}</ref>

During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the highway served as a conduit for the cultural and technological advancements brought by envoys and monks from the mainland. It played a pivotal role in transmitting the advanced culture and Buddhism that became foundational to Japanese civilization.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

In the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages]], it remained a vital route as part of the {{ill|Ise Road|ja|伊勢街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢参宮街道|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}. By 1890, it had been designated a prefectural road, and in 1974, it became part of [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]].{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=52}} Thus, the highway has been in continuous use from the Asuka period to the present.

* '''{{ill|Daisen Kofun|fi|Daisenry%C5%8D-kofun|pt|Daisenry%C5%8D-Kofun|fr|Kofun Daisenry%C5%8D|es|Daisenry%C5%8D-Kofun|en|Daisen Kofun|ca|Kofun Daisenry%C5%8D|ko|%EB%8B%A4%EC%9D%B4%EC%84%BC%EB%A6%AD %EA%B3%A0%EB%B6%84|zh|%E5%A4%A7%E4%BB%99%E9%99%B5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|lt=Emperor Nintoku's Mausoleum|ja|仁徳天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''
* '''{{ill|Empress Suiko's Mausoleum|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|lt=Empress Suiko's Mausoleum (Yamada Takatsuka Kofun)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko's Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}'''

The highway's name derives from the Takenouchi settlement near the {{ill|Takenouchi Pass|ja|竹内峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Pass|1=Takenouchi Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598871}}, located in Katsuragi.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

== History ==

The Takenouchi Highway is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] under the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] in 613 CE. It describes the establishment of a "grand road" connecting Naniwa (modern [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]]) to the capital in Asuka.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=道と路がわかる辞典|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha}}</ref> One theory suggests that [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] commissioned the highway to facilitate diplomatic exchanges with the mainland, especially for envoys such as [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] during missions to the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]].<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=道路location map びっくり!博学知識|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha}}</ref>

During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the highway served as a conduit for the cultural and technological advancements brought by envoys and monks from the mainland. It played a pivotal role in transmitting the advanced culture and Buddhism that became foundational to Japanese civilization.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

In the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages]], it remained a vital route as part of the {{ill|Ise Road|ja|伊勢街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢参宮街道|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}. By 1890, it had been designated a prefectural road, and in 1974, it became part of [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]].{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=52}} Thus, the highway has been in continuous use from the Asuka period to the present.

The left side is part of [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]], while the right side shows the old highway.]]

'''Takenouchi Highway''' (竹内街道) is the oldest ancient government road in Japan, stretching from [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] to the area near [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] in [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. Historically, it was known as '''Tajihi Road''' (丹比道).<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Osaka no Kaidō (大阪の街道)|year=1989|publisher=Shōraisha|page=66}}</ref>

From the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]] to the vicinity of the Takenouchi settlement in Katsuragi, much of the route was designated as [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]] in 1974. In 2017, it became the first [[:en:Japan Heritage|Japan Heritage]] site in Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|title=Japan Heritage Recognition! Japan's Oldest National Route "Takenouchi Highway and Yokooji"|url=http://www.saikonokandou.com/2017/04/1470/|website=Saikonokandou.com|accessdate=2019-04-04}}</ref> Additionally, in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi]], major events such as the "Taishi Sacred Lantern Gathering" in spring and the "Takenouchi Highway Lantern Festival" in autumn are held annually.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/event_info/nenkangyouji1/1534900110524.html|title=FY2018 Annual Events|website=Taishi Town Official Website|publisher=[[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi]]|accessdate=2019-04-07}}</ref>

== Overview ==

The Takenouchi Highway is an ancient arterial road that traverses the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, connecting [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] with [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]. It is regarded as Japan's oldest {{ill|ancient highway|ja|古代官道|1=kandō|lt=Ancient Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient highway|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaidō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官道|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452141}}. Extending approximately 26 kilometers from the Daishōji area of Sakai to Nagao Shrine in Katsuragi (23 kilometers within Osaka Prefecture), it retains remnants of its original path.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=Road Map: Amazing Knowledge Encyclopedia (道路location map びっくり!博学知識)|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha|series=Kawade Yume Bunko|pages=52–53|isbn=4-309-49566-4}}</ref> Though the bustling cityscapes of Naniwa and Asuka have obscured its original width (once up to 30 meters during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]), the highway's historical significance as a transportation and cultural transmission route remains evident through its proximity to numerous ancient Kofuns, such as.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=Dictionary of Roads and Paths (道と路がわかる辞典)|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha|isbn=4-534-03315-X}}</ref>

The highway's name derives from the Takenouchi settlement near the {{ill|Takenouchi Pass|ja|竹内峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Pass|1=Takenouchi Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598871}}, located in Katsuragi.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

== History ==

The Takenouchi Highway is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] under the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] in 613 CE. It describes the establishment of a "grand road" connecting Naniwa (modern [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]]) to the capital in Asuka.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=道と路がわかる辞典|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha}}</ref> One theory suggests that [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] commissioned the highway to facilitate diplomatic exchanges with the mainland, especially for envoys such as [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] during missions to the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]].<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=道路location map びっくり!博学知識|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha}}</ref>

During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the highway served as a conduit for the cultural and technological advancements brought by envoys and monks from the mainland. It played a pivotal role in transmitting the advanced culture and Buddhism that became foundational to Japanese civilization.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

In the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages]], it remained a vital route as part of the {{ill|Ise Road|ja|伊勢街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢参宮街道|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}. The left side is part of [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]], while the right side shows the old highway.]]

'''Takenouchi Highway''' (竹内街道) is the oldest ancient government road in Japan, stretching from [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] to the area near [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] in [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. Historically, it was known as '''Tajihi Road''' (丹比道).<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Osaka no Kaidō (大阪の街道)|year=1989|publisher=Shōraisha|page=66}}</ref>

From the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]] to the vicinity of the Takenouchi settlement in Katsuragi, much of the route was designated as [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]] in 1974. In 2017, it became the first [[:en:Japan Heritage|Japan Heritage]] site in Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|title=Japan Heritage Recognition! Japan's Oldest National Route "Takenouchi Highway and Yokooji"|url=http://www.saikonokandou.com/2017/04/1470/|website=Saikonokandou.com|accessdate=2019-04-04}}</ref> Additionally, in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi]], major events such as the "Taishi Sacred Lantern Gathering" in spring and the "Takenouchi Highway Lantern Festival" in autumn are held annually.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/event_info/nenkangyouji1/1534900110524.html|title=FY2018 Annual Events|website=Taishi Town Official Website|publisher=[[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi]]|accessdate=2019-04-07}}</ref>

== Overview ==

The Takenouchi Highway is an ancient arterial road that traverses the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, connecting [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] with [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]. It is regarded as Japan's oldest {{ill|ancient highway|ja|古代官道|1=kandō|lt=Ancient Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient highway|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaidō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官道|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452141}}. Extending approximately 26 kilometers from the Daishōji area of Sakai to Nagao Shrine in Katsuragi (23 kilometers within Osaka Prefecture), it retains remnants of its original path.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=Road Map: Amazing Knowledge Encyclopedia (道路location map びっくり!博学知識)|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha|series=Kawade Yume Bunko|pages=52–53|isbn=4-309-49566-4}}</ref> Though the bustling cityscapes of Naniwa and Asuka have obscured its original width (once up to 30 meters during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]), the highway's historical significance as a transportation and cultural transmission route remains evident through its proximity to numerous ancient Kofuns, such as.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=Dictionary of Roads and Paths (道と路がわかる辞典)|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha|isbn=4-534-03315-X}}</ref>

* '''{{ill|Kondayama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|lt=Emperor Ōjin's Mausoleum|ja|応神天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}'''
* '''{{ill|Daisen Kofun|fi|Daisenry%C5%8D-kofun|pt|Daisenry%C5%8D-Kofun|fr|Kofun Daisenry%C5%8D|es|Daisenry%C5%8D-Kofun|en|Daisen Kofun|ca|Kofun Daisenry%C5%8D|ko|%EB%8B%A4%EC%9D%B4%EC%84%BC%EB%A6%AD %EA%B3%A0%EB%B6%84|zh|%E5%A4%A7%E4%BB%99%E9%99%B5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|lt=Emperor Nintoku's Mausoleum|ja|仁徳天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''
* '''{{ill|Empress Suiko's Mausoleum|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|lt=Empress Suiko's Mausoleum (Yamada Takatsuka Kofun)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko's Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}'''

The highway's name derives from the Takenouchi settlement near the {{ill|Takenouchi Pass|ja|竹内峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Pass|1=Takenouchi Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598871}}, located in Katsuragi.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

== History ==

The Takenouchi Highway is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] under the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] in 613 CE. It describes the establishment of a "grand road" connecting Naniwa (modern [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]]) to the capital in Asuka.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=道と路がわかる辞典|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha}}</ref> One theory suggests that [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] commissioned the highway to facilitate diplomatic exchanges with the mainland, especially for envoys such as [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] during missions to the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]].<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=道路location map びっくり!博学知識|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha}}</ref>

During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the highway served as a conduit for the cultural and technological advancements brought by envoys and monks from the mainland. It played a pivotal role in transmitting the advanced culture and Buddhism that became foundational to Japanese civilization.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

In the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages]], it remained a vital route as part of the {{ill|Ise Road|ja|伊勢街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢参宮街道|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}. By 1890, it had been designated a prefectural road, and in 1974, it became part of [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]].{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=52}} Thus, the highway has been in continuous use from the Asuka period to the present.

== Overview ==

The Takenouchi Highway is an ancient arterial road that traverses the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, connecting [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] with [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]. It is regarded as Japan's oldest {{ill|ancient highway|ja|古代官道|1=kandō|lt=Ancient Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient highway|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaidō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官道|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452141}}. Extending approximately 26 kilometers from the Daishōji area of Sakai to Nagao Shrine in Katsuragi (23 kilometers within Osaka Prefecture), it retains remnants of its original path.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=Road Map: Amazing Knowledge Encyclopedia (道路location map びっくり!博学知識)|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha|series=Kawade Yume Bunko|pages=52–53|isbn=4-309-49566-4}}</ref> Though the bustling cityscapes of Naniwa and Asuka have obscured its original width (once up to 30 meters during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]), the highway's historical significance as a transportation and cultural transmission route remains evident through its proximity to numerous ancient Kofuns, such as.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=Dictionary of Roads and Paths (道と路がわかる辞典)|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha|isbn=4-534-03315-X}}</ref>

* '''{{ill|Kondayama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|lt=Emperor Ōjin's Mausoleum|ja|応神天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}'''
* '''{{ill|Daisen Kofun|fi|Daisenry%C5%8D-kofun|pt|Daisenry%C5%8D-Kofun|fr|Kofun Daisenry%C5%8D|es|Daisenry%C5%8D-Kofun|en|Daisen Kofun|ca|Kofun Daisenry%C5%8D|ko|%EB%8B%A4%EC%9D%B4%EC%84%BC%EB%A6%AD %EA%B3%A0%EB%B6%84|zh|%E5%A4%A7%E4%BB%99%E9%99%B5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|lt=Emperor Nintoku's Mausoleum|ja|仁徳天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''
* '''{{ill|Empress Suiko's Mausoleum|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|lt=Empress Suiko's Mausoleum (Yamada Takatsuka Kofun)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko's Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}'''

The highway's name derives from the Takenouchi settlement near the {{ill|Takenouchi Pass|ja|竹内峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Pass|1=Takenouchi Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598871}}, located in Katsuragi.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

== History ==

The Takenouchi Highway is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] under the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] in 613 CE. It describes the establishment of a "grand road" connecting Naniwa (modern [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]]) to the capital in Asuka.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=道と路がわかる辞典|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha}}</ref> One theory suggests that [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] commissioned the highway to facilitate diplomatic exchanges with the mainland, especially for envoys such as [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] during missions to the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]].<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=道路location map びっくり!博学知識|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha}}</ref>

During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the highway served as a conduit for the cultural and technological advancements brought by envoys and monks from the mainland. It played a pivotal role in transmitting the advanced culture and Buddhism that became foundational to Japanese civilization.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

In the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages]], it remained a vital route as part of the {{ill|Ise Road|ja|伊勢街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢参宮街道|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}. By 1890, it had been designated a prefectural road, and in 1974, it became part of [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]].{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=52}} Thus, the highway has been in continuous use from the Asuka period to the present.

The left side is part of [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]], while the right side shows the old highway.]]

'''Takenouchi Highway''' (竹内街道) is the oldest ancient government road in Japan, stretching from [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] to the area near [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] in [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. Historically, it was known as '''Tajihi Road''' (丹比道).<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Osaka no Kaidō (大阪の街道)|year=1989|publisher=Shōraisha|page=66}}</ref>

From the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]] to the vicinity of the Takenouchi settlement in Katsuragi, much of the route was designated as [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]] in 1974. In 2017, it became the first [[:en:Japan Heritage|Japan Heritage]] site in Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|title=Japan Heritage Recognition! Japan's Oldest National Route "Takenouchi Highway and Yokooji"|url=http://www.saikonokandou.com/2017/04/1470/|website=Saikonokandou.com|accessdate=2019-04-04}}</ref> Additionally, in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi]], major events such as the "Taishi Sacred Lantern Gathering" in spring and the "Takenouchi Highway Lantern Festival" in autumn are held annually.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/event_info/nenkangyouji1/1534900110524.html|title=FY2018 Annual Events|website=Taishi Town Official Website|publisher=[[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi]]|accessdate=2019-04-07}}</ref>

It is regarded as Japan's oldest {{ill|ancient highway|ja|古代官道|1=kandō|lt=Ancient Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient highway|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaidō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官道|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452141}}. During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the highway served as a conduit for the cultural and technological advancements brought by envoys and monks from the mainland. It played a pivotal role in transmitting the advanced culture and Buddhism that became foundational to Japanese civilization.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

In the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages]], it remained a vital route as part of the {{ill|Ise Road|ja|伊勢街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢参宮街道|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}. By 1890, it had been designated a prefectural road, and in 1974, it became part of [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]].{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=52}} Thus, the highway has been in continuous use from the Asuka period to the present.

The left side is part of [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]], while the right side shows the old highway.]]

'''Takenouchi Highway''' (竹内街道) is the oldest ancient government road in Japan, stretching from [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] to the area near [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] in [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. Historically, it was known as '''Tajihi Road''' (丹比道).<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Osaka no Kaidō (大阪の街道)|year=1989|publisher=Shōraisha|page=66}}</ref>

From the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]] to the vicinity of the Takenouchi settlement in Katsuragi, much of the route was designated as [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]] in 1974. In 2017, it became the first [[:en:Japan Heritage|Japan Heritage]] site in Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|title=Japan Heritage Recognition! Japan's Oldest National Route "Takenouchi Highway and Yokooji"|url=http://www.saikonokandou.com/2017/04/1470/|website=Saikonokandou.com|accessdate=2019-04-04}}</ref> Additionally, in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi]], major events such as the "Taishi Sacred Lantern Gathering" in spring and the "Takenouchi Highway Lantern Festival" in autumn are held annually.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/event_info/nenkangyouji1/1534900110524.html|title=FY2018 Annual Events|website=Taishi Town Official Website|publisher=[[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi]]|accessdate=2019-04-07}}</ref>

== Overview ==

The Takenouchi Highway is an ancient arterial road that traverses the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, connecting [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] with [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]. It is regarded as Japan's oldest {{ill|ancient highway|ja|古代官道|1=kandō|lt=Ancient Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient highway|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaidō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官道|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452141}}. Extending approximately 26 kilometers from the Daishōji area of Sakai to Nagao Shrine in Katsuragi (23 kilometers within Osaka Prefecture), it retains remnants of its original path.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=Road Map: Amazing Knowledge Encyclopedia (道路location map びっくり!博学知識)|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha|series=Kawade Yume Bunko|pages=52–53|isbn=4-309-49566-4}}</ref> Though the bustling cityscapes of Naniwa and Asuka have obscured its original width (once up to 30 meters during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]), the highway's historical significance as a transportation and cultural transmission route remains evident through its proximity to numerous ancient Kofuns, such as.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=Dictionary of Roads and Paths (道と路がわかる辞典)|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha|isbn=4-534-03315-X}}</ref>

* '''{{ill|Kondayama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|lt=Emperor Ōjin's Mausoleum|ja|応神天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}'''
* '''{{ill|Daisen Kofun|fi|Daisenry%C5%8D-kofun|pt|Daisenry%C5%8D-Kofun|fr|Kofun Daisenry%C5%8D|es|Daisenry%C5%8D-Kofun|en|Daisen Kofun|ca|Kofun Daisenry%C5%8D|ko|%EB%8B%A4%EC%9D%B4%EC%84%BC%EB%A6%AD %EA%B3%A0%EB%B6%84|zh|%E5%A4%A7%E4%BB%99%E9%99%B5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|lt=Emperor Nintoku's Mausoleum|ja|仁徳天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''
* '''{{ill|Empress Suiko's Mausoleum|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|lt=Empress Suiko's Mausoleum (Yamada Takatsuka Kofun)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko's Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}'''

The highway's name derives from the Takenouchi settlement near the {{ill|Takenouchi Pass|ja|竹内峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Pass|1=Takenouchi Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598871}}, located in Katsuragi.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

== History ==

The Takenouchi Highway is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] under the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] in 613 CE. It describes the establishment of a "grand road" connecting Naniwa (modern [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]]) to the capital in Asuka.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=道と路がわかる辞典|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha}}</ref> One theory suggests that [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] commissioned the highway to facilitate diplomatic exchanges with the mainland, especially for envoys such as [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] during missions to the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]].<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=道路location map びっくり!博学知識|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha}}</ref>

During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the highway served as a conduit for the cultural and technological advancements brought by envoys and monks from the mainland. It played a pivotal role in transmitting the advanced culture and Buddhism that became foundational to Japanese civilization.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

In the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages]], it remained a vital route as part of the {{ill|Ise Road|ja|伊勢街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢参宮街道|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}. By 1890, it had been designated a prefectural road, and in 1974, it became part of [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]].{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=52}} Thus, the highway has been in continuous use from the Asuka period to the present.

'''Takenouchi Highway''' (竹内街道) is the oldest ancient government road in Japan, stretching from [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]] in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] to the area near [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] in [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. Historically, it was known as '''Tajihi Road''' (丹比道).<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Osaka no Kaidō (大阪の街道)|year=1989|publisher=Shōraisha|page=66}}</ref>

From the {{ill|Shiratori Intersection|ja|白鳥交差点|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Intersection|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino]] to the vicinity of the Takenouchi settlement in Katsuragi, much of the route was designated as [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]] in 1974. In 2017, it became the first [[:en:Japan Heritage|Japan Heritage]] site in Osaka.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|title=Japan Heritage Recognition! Japan's Oldest National Route "Takenouchi Highway and Yokooji"|url=http://www.saikonokandou.com/2017/04/1470/|website=Saikonokandou.com|accessdate=2019-04-04}}</ref> Additionally, in [[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi]], major events such as the "Taishi Sacred Lantern Gathering" in spring and the "Takenouchi Highway Lantern Festival" in autumn are held annually.<ref>{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=http://www.town.taishi.osaka.jp/event_info/nenkangyouji1/1534900110524.html|title=FY2018 Annual Events|website=Taishi Town Official Website|publisher=[[:en:Taishi, Osaka|Taishi]]|accessdate=2019-04-07}}</ref>

== Overview ==

The Takenouchi Highway is an ancient arterial road that traverses the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Nijō|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Nijō|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}, connecting [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] with [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]. It is regarded as Japan's oldest {{ill|ancient highway|ja|古代官道|1=kandō|lt=Ancient Kaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient highway|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaidō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 官道|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452141}}. Extending approximately 26 kilometers from the Daishōji area of Sakai to Nagao Shrine in Katsuragi (23 kilometers within Osaka Prefecture), it retains remnants of its original path.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=Road Map: Amazing Knowledge Encyclopedia (道路location map びっくり!博学知識)|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha|series=Kawade Yume Bunko|pages=52–53|isbn=4-309-49566-4}}</ref> Though the bustling cityscapes of Naniwa and Asuka have obscured its original width (once up to 30 meters during the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]), the highway's historical significance as a transportation and cultural transmission route remains evident through its proximity to numerous ancient Kofuns, such as.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=Dictionary of Roads and Paths (道と路がわかる辞典)|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha|isbn=4-534-03315-X}}</ref>

* '''{{ill|Kondayama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|lt=Emperor Ōjin's Mausoleum|ja|応神天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}'''
* '''{{ill|Daisen Kofun|fi|Daisenry%C5%8D-kofun|pt|Daisenry%C5%8D-Kofun|fr|Kofun Daisenry%C5%8D|es|Daisenry%C5%8D-Kofun|en|Daisen Kofun|ca|Kofun Daisenry%C5%8D|ko|%EB%8B%A4%EC%9D%B4%EC%84%BC%EB%A6%AD %EA%B3%A0%EB%B6%84|zh|%E5%A4%A7%E4%BB%99%E9%99%B5%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|lt=Emperor Nintoku's Mausoleum|ja|仁徳天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'''
* '''{{ill|Empress Suiko's Mausoleum|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|lt=Empress Suiko's Mausoleum (Yamada Takatsuka Kofun)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko's Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}'''

The highway's name derives from the Takenouchi settlement near the {{ill|Takenouchi Pass|ja|竹内峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Pass|1=Takenouchi Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598871}}, located in Katsuragi.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

== History ==

The Takenouchi Highway is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] under the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] in 613 CE. It describes the establishment of a "grand road" connecting Naniwa (modern [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]]) to the capital in Asuka.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=道と路がわかる辞典|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha}}</ref> One theory suggests that [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] commissioned the highway to facilitate diplomatic exchanges with the mainland, especially for envoys such as [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] during missions to the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]].<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=道路location map びっくり!博学知識|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha}}</ref>

During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the highway served as a conduit for the cultural and technological advancements brought by envoys and monks from the mainland. It played a pivotal role in transmitting the advanced culture and Buddhism that became foundational to Japanese civilization.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

In the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages]], it remained a vital route as part of the {{ill|Ise Road|ja|伊勢街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢参宮街道|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}. By 1890, it had been designated a prefectural road, and in 1974, it became part of [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]].{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=52}} Thus, the highway has been in continuous use from the Asuka period to the present.

* '''{{ill|Empress Suiko's Mausoleum|ja|山田高塚古墳|1=Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|lt=Empress Suiko's Mausoleum (Yamada Takatsuka Kofun)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko's Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}}'''

The highway's name derives from the Takenouchi settlement near the {{ill|Takenouchi Pass|ja|竹内峠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Pass|1=Takenouchi Pass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598871}}, located in Katsuragi.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

== History ==

The Takenouchi Highway is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] under the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] in 613 CE. It describes the establishment of a "grand road" connecting Naniwa (modern [[:en:Osaka|Osaka]]) to the capital in Asuka.<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Kenji Asai|date=2001|title=道と路がわかる辞典|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyō Shuppansha}}</ref> One theory suggests that [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] commissioned the highway to facilitate diplomatic exchanges with the mainland, especially for envoys such as [[:en:Ono no Imoko|Ono no Imoko]] during missions to the [[:en:Sui dynasty|Sui dynasty]].<ref>{{Cite book|lang=ja|author=Rom International (ed.)|date=2005|title=道路location map びっくり!博学知識|publisher=Kawade Shobō Shinsha}}</ref>

During the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], the highway served as a conduit for the cultural and technological advancements brought by envoys and monks from the mainland. It played a pivotal role in transmitting the advanced culture and Buddhism that became foundational to Japanese civilization.{{sfn|Kenji Asai|2001}}

In the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages]], it remained a vital route as part of the {{ill|Ise Road|ja|伊勢街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢参宮街道|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}. By 1890, it had been designated a prefectural road, and in 1974, it became part of [[:en:Japan National Route 166|Japan National Route 166]].{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=52}} Thus, the highway has been in continuous use from the Asuka period to the present.
